tensions.actual: v1.5+dev: Source ahead of installed binary. Conceptual foundation complete (#13 resolved — 13/14 children, all subsystems delivered). CLI complete (#10 resolved — 5 reading commands, all operative gestures, JSON on everything, help by framework). Three interface surfaces operational: TUI (survey V1.3, deck, logbase Phase 1, FrankenSearch palette, undo/redo), CLI (logbase + structural gestures + search + undo), MCP (35+ tools). FrankenSuite: FNX graph substrate, fsqlite MVCC, FrankenSearch core, ftui structural foundation. User-defined signal thresholds. Gesture undo. Quint formal specifications. Cooperative dynamics unexplored.

tensions.actual: Authority, provenance, and multi-player identity surfaced as design questions through community issue evaluation. The operative-not-managerial distinction is now canonized in the conceptual foundation — werk serves operations (closing gaps), not management (coordinating existing structure). This sharpens the multi-participant question: cooperation in werk means shared commitment to closing gaps honestly, not coordination topology. The addressing/sessions/gestures exploration (designs/addressing-sessions-gestures.md) is where the multi-player foundation lives — sessions carry authorship, gestures carry provenance, deep addressability makes everything referenceable across participants.
tensions.desired: a clear structural model exists for how multiple participants hold and evolve shared tension fields

tensions.actual: All speculative dynamics gutted. sd-core computes only honest facts: urgency (elapsed fraction of horizon), horizon drift (pattern of horizon changes), gap_magnitude (binary — has gap or not), temporal signals (implied window, sequencing pressure, critical path, containment violation), closure progress. Net -15,095 lines across 28 files. 881 tests pass. DynamicsEngine simplified to Store wrapper with parent snapshot capture. CLI JSON output drops dynamics field — shows temporal signals, urgency, overdue, closure instead. strsim dependency removed. TUI updated to render without speculative dynamics. Consider renaming sd-core to werk-core as optional follow-up.

No speculative dynamics remain. Consider renaming sd-core to werk-core.

tensions.actual: Sessions are process-scoped by design. begin_gesture() creates sessionless gestures (CLI, agent). begin_gesture_in_session(session_id) associates with a specific session (TUI). active_session() query still exists for TUI use, but CLI/agent paths never call it. Multiple TUI panes = multiple independent sessions. Agent mutations = sessionless gestures. This is architecturally settled. Remaining: TUI needs to call start_session on open and end_session on close.
tensions.desired: session lifecycle is clear — TUI instances manage their own sessions, CLI commands operate sessionless, agent mutations are sessionless gestures. Multiple concurrent TUI panes are independent sessions.

tensions.actual: decided: mutation log is sufficient, no persistence layer. Signals remain a pure function of (mutations, now). Scaling math shows no near-term concern (10K mutations = sub-second). If hooks (#137) are later needed, compute-on-write at mutation time rather than a snapshot table. Purity guarantee — no hidden state that could diverge — is worth more than pre-computed convenience. Consistent with Standard of Measurement: instrument computes from what user provided.
tensions.desired: should signal state be persisted separately or is the mutation log sufficient as the single source of truth for computing signals at any point in time

tensions.actual: decided: add --robot as a separate flag (additive, not breaking --json). Envelope pattern {ok, data, error, elapsed_ms} consistent with btv/cass/dcg/sc ecosystem. Low priority — MCP is the primary agent surface with its own protocol envelope. --robot serves CLI-as-agent-tool case, which is secondary. Defer implementation until an agent integration actually needs it.
tensions.desired: should the CLI adopt a --robot envelope pattern (ok/data/error/elapsed_ms) for agent consumption beyond the current --json raw data output

tensions.actual: decided: attention tracking is out of scope. Tracking 'when user last looked' introduces an instrument-originated standard of measurement, violating principle #10. Creates a managerial surveillance dynamic contradicting operative identity. Existing mechanisms suffice: --changed (user-supplied timeframe) and --stale (user-supplied threshold) are both anchored to user standards.
tensions.desired: does temporal attention tracking (when did the user last look, what changed since) belong in the signal system or is it a separate concern

tensions.actual: decided: deferred by design. Event types already exist (UrgencyThresholdCrossed, HorizonDriftDetected) but no consumer exists. No daemon, no webhook infra, MCP queries on demand. When a consumer appears (TUI live updates, werk watch), the path is clear: compute-on-write in grammar layer, emit existing event types, subscribe in consumer. Building infrastructure without a consumer violates design discipline.

tensions.actual: All five phases shipped. werk-shared::cli_display module owns the visual vocabulary (20-glyph registry + 7-role Palette). Universal color via output.palette() in tree, show, list, log, stats, insights. Tree has rich two-line root layout with ╭─/╰─ zones in interactive ≥80-col terminals; v1.5 compact path on pipes/NO_COLOR/--compact (byte-identical to baseline). Show has bold-cyan section headers, signal lines with semantic color. List dims completed rows, colors signal glyphs by category. Log epochs render as cyan zones with magenta testimony desire snapshots. Stats has bold headers, resolved-green vitals, danger-red overdue. Insights bar charts colored. Mutation echo (--show-after) on reality/desire/resolve/release: human mode default-on, JSON additive. Contextual footer hints on tree/show/list/log/stats. All 298 werk integration tests still pass, plus 4 new Palette unit tests.
tensions.desired: the werk CLI has a world-class visual display — a coherent visual language where color, glyphs, line weight, zone boundaries, and breathing room turn terminal output into an instrument surface as precise as musical notation

tensions.actual: GitButler's --json --status-after returns {result, status} in one blob. werk --json returns minimal confirmation, requiring a second call.
tensions.desired: JSON mutation responses include the updated tension view — one round-trip for agents via combined result+show payload

tensions.actual: cli_display module landed with glyph registry (20 canonical glyphs) and Palette struct (7 color roles + bold). tree.rs imports glyph constants from the registry and constructs a Palette at entry. Output is byte-identical to pre-refactor baseline. All 298 werk integration tests pass, plus 4 new Palette unit tests.
tensions.desired: Phase 1: shared cli_display module — glyph registry, color palette, Palette struct with NO_COLOR/non-TTY detection, all constants centralized in werk-shared

tensions.actual: Output struct owns the shared Palette; TTY/NO_COLOR/JSON detection centralized at construction. tree.rs, show.rs, list.rs, and output.rs all route color through output.palette(). Inline glyph literals deleted from show.rs (signal section) and list.rs (signal computation + footer legend). Completed rows in list dim via body_colorize. OVERDUE is palette.bold(palette.danger). Non-TTY output byte-identical for tree; show and list visually identical in non-TTY (label spacing preserved). log, stats, survey deferred to Phase 5 alongside their full redesign. All 298 werk tests pass.
tensions.desired: Phase 2: universal color — every command that outputs human text goes through the shared palette. show, list, stats, log, survey all get semantic color

tensions.actual: Rich two-line root layout with ╭─/╰─ zone boundaries, signal lines below tensions with warnings (OVERDUE, EXCEEDS_PARENT, PRESSURE on their own indented bold-red line), empty rail lines between depth-1 siblings that have children (breathing room), empty line between root zones. --compact flag forces v1.5 layout; auto-fallback under 80 cols, non-TTY, NO_COLOR, or --json. Dispatch is palette.is_enabled() && term_width >= 80 && !compact. Compact path byte-identical to baseline. All 298 werk tests pass.
tensions.desired: Phase 3: tree redesign — zone boundaries on roots, two-line root layout (desire first, metadata below), signal lines, depth-sensitive breathing room, dimmed chrome

tensions.actual: show section headers now bold + cyan (Signals:, Frontier:, Children:, Activity:, Epochs:, Ancestors:, Siblings:, Engagement:). New hints helper prints contextual one-line footer suggestions on tree/show/list, dim only in interactive terminals (no-op when palette disabled). All 298 werk tests pass; existing JSON consumers see no breaking change. List column alignment, --signals band separators, and move/position/snooze echo deferred.

tensions.actual: werk hooks install offers 5 pre-commit-only shipped hooks (flush, readme-tree, auto-stage, guard-delete, audit-log). No hooks exist for session boundaries, workspace entry, or structural transitions. The hook system has no lifecycle awareness beyond commit time.

tensions.actual: No standard mechanism exists for an LLM to discover that a project uses werk. CLAUDE.md may mention werk but there is no established pointer pattern. No assurance that werk's JSON output is sufficient for LLM reasoning. werk does not make itself discoverable to agents.
tensions.desired: werk ensures LLMs work well with it — a clean pointer in agent config files points to werk, and werk's query surface (--json on every command, structured output) gives LLMs what they need without werk owning project documentation

tensions.actual: design resolved through reasoning session. Key decisions: (1) the global space is a normal space at ~/.werk/ with registry awareness — not a new ontological entity, (2) spaces are unified — directory-bound or pure, same struct with optional dir binding, (3) 'space' is the term (not workspace, domain, field), (4) discovery: three-tier (explicit flag > UP walk > global fallback), (5) cross-space references are generalized edges (#154), (6) the global space's command center IS the aggregate view across spaces (#90 at wider scope). No implementation yet.
tensions.desired: the global space exists — a space at ~/.werk/ that serves as the registry of all other spaces, holds its own general-purpose tensions, and provides the aggregate command center view across all registered spaces. Spaces are unified: same struct, same operations, same addressing, with an optional directory binding. CLI: werk -g (global), werk -w <name> (specific space), bare werk (location-detected or global fallback). Cross-space addressing and references are edge types within the generalized edge substrate (#154).
